summary: Clear 48‑track soundboard mix captures an above‑average 1991 show highlighted by a stellar Eyes of the World, Walkin' Blues and a year‑best Truckin'.
review: The SBD (soundboard) source provides a dynamic, multi‑track clarity that lets the band’s interplay shine, despite occasional artificial audience level jumps. Musically the concert is above average for the era – the first set grooves with an exciting Eyes of the World and Walkin' Blues, while the second set delivers a standout Truckin' and strong jams, though Saint and Space fall a bit flat.

ai_rating.rationale: The detailed review praises both the performance (above‑average show with several highlights) and the recording (clear multi‑track sound), yielding a strong combined score; only one substantive review limits confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: 48‑track SBD mix offers excellent image and clarity of a multitrack, though audience volume is raised artificially in the OFF sections; overall dynamic and well balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls strong with tight two‑key interplay; second set varies – smooth jams and a superb Truckin' contrast with a few flatter moments, ending on a solid Sugar Magnolia.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Delivers great solos (notably on Loose Lucy) and leads several Dark Star teases.
band_member_comments.Phil: Sounds in good form throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Guides early Dark Star tease and later unique UJB jam, keeping energy high.
band_member_comments.Keys: Dual‑keyboard setup shines; Brent (and guest) provide rich textures.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers have a strong night, delivering an absorbing Drum segment.
